vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: The finding involves correcting file permissions on /etc/gshadow, which is a standard STIG file permission hardening task and does not affect authentication services or critical system boot components.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential misconfiguration if incorrect permissions are applied; May affect group membership visibility but not system access
